proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_004 (3-level categorical) is associated with objective_response, with higher levels predicting lower response.
feature_014 (binary) is associated with objective_response, with feature_014=1 predicting higher response.
feature_001 (binary) is associated with objective_response, with feature_001=1 predicting higher response.
feature_021 (binary) is associated with objective_response, with feature_021=1 predicting higher response.

analyses.code: ttest_ind on feature_004 by objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_004 mean is 0.797 in responders vs 0.954 in non-responders (Welch t-test). Higher feature_004 levels have lower response: rr=0.231 (level 0), 0.174 (level 1), 0.129 (level 2).

analyses.code: chi-square: feature_014 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_014=1 (n=15014) has marginal response rate 0.235 vs 0.167 in feature_014=0; OR=1.53.

analyses.code: chi-square: feature_001 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_001=1 (n=20056) has response rate 0.222 vs 0.165 in feature_001=0; OR=1.43.

analyses.code: chi-square: feature_021 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_021=1 (n=20020) has response rate 0.221 vs 0.166 in feature_021=0; OR=1.41.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher feature_020 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_024 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_022 is associated with lower objective_response.
Higher feature_029 is associated with higher objective_response.
Higher feature_027 is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_020 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_020 is 3.42 in responders vs 3.93 in non-responders (diff=-0.51).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_024 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_024 is 58.5 in responders vs 60.3 in non-responders (diff=-1.80).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_022 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_022 is 19.27 in responders vs 20.39 in non-responders (diff=-1.11).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_029 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_029 is 3.825 in responders vs 3.796 in non-responders (diff=+0.030).

analyses.code: Welch t-test on feature_027 by response
analyses.result_summary: Mean feature_027 is 5.69 in responders vs 6.17 in non-responders (diff=-0.48).

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026=1 is associated with lower objective_response.
feature_009=1 is associated with lower objective_response.

analyses.code: chi-square: feature_026 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_026=1 (n=7462) has response 0.166 vs 0.191 in feature_026=0; OR=0.86.

analyses.code: chi-square: feature_009 vs objective_response
analyses.result_summary: feature_009=1 (n=4985) has response 0.165 vs 0.190 in feature_009=0; OR=0.85.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After mutual adjustment in a multivariable logistic model, feature_014, feature_001, feature_021 each remain independent positive predictors of objective_response, while feature_004, feature_026, feature_009, feature_020, feature_024 remain independent negative predictors.

analyses.code: Logit(objective_response ~ all 35 features); continuous standardized.
analyses.result_summary: Pseudo-R^2=0.032. Significant positive coefs (log-odds): feature_014 +0.436 (p<1e-50), feature_001 +0.374 (p<1e-50), feature_021 +0.362 (p<1e-50), feature_029 +0.061 (p<1e-6), feature_007 +0.026 (p=0.023). Significant negative coefs: feature_004 -0.362 (p<1e-50), feature_026 -0.169 (p<1e-6), feature_009 -0.188 (p<1e-5), feature_020 -0.142 (p<1e-30), feature_024 -0.100 (p<1e-16), feature_027 -0.060 (p<1e-5), feature_022 -0.048 (p=1e-4). Other features (feature_011, feature_010, feature_005, feature_030, feature_018, feature_008 etc.) are non-significant.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Treatment effect of feature_014 on objective_response is heterogeneous across feature_001 and feature_021 strata, with the largest effect when both feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1.

analyses.code: Stratified Fisher exact for feature_014 by feature_001 x feature_021 strata
analyses.result_summary: Within feature_001=0 & feature_021=0: tx response 0.160 vs ctrl 0.172 (OR=0.92). Within feature_001=0 & feature_021=1: tx 0.157 vs 0.162 (OR=0.96). Within feature_001=1 & feature_021=0: tx 0.160 vs 0.164 (OR=0.97). Within feature_001=1 & feature_021=1: tx 0.633 vs 0.169 (OR=8.48, p<1e-300). Treatment effect is dramatically concentrated in the dual-positive stratum.

proposed_hypotheses.text: A three-way interaction term feature_014 x feature_001 x feature_021 is significant in a logistic model, supporting the dual-biomarker requirement for benefit.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ feature_014 + feature_001 + feature_021 + all 2-way + 3-way)
analyses.result_summary: Three-way interaction coefficient = +2.126 (z=20.4, p<1e-50). All main effects and 2-way interaction terms are essentially zero or trivially small once the 3-way term is included; the response effect is nearly entirely captured by the joint feature_014 x feature_001 x feature_021 indicator.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the dual-biomarker-positive subgroup (feature_001=1 & feature_021=1), feature_026=1 abrogates the treatment benefit of feature_014.
Within the dual-biomarker-positive subgroup, feature_009=1 abrogates the treatment benefit of feature_014.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ tx + feature_026 + tx*feature_026) inside feature_001=1 & feature_021=1
analyses.result_summary: Within dual-positive (n=8013): tx*feature_026 interaction coefficient = -2.553 (p~1e-49). Treatment effect by feature_026: feature_026=0 → tx 0.717 vs 0.166 (large benefit); feature_026=1 → tx 0.184 vs 0.186 (no benefit).

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ tx + feature_009 + tx*feature_009) inside feature_001=1 & feature_021=1
analyses.result_summary: Within dual-positive: tx*feature_009 interaction coefficient = -2.281 (p~1e-30). Treatment effect by feature_009: feature_009=0 → tx 0.691 vs 0.169; feature_009=1 → tx 0.188 vs 0.171.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The feature_014 treatment effect is concentrated in the four-feature subgroup defined by feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0; outside this subgroup the treatment has no effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: Stratified Fisher exact for feature_014 inside vs outside the 4-feature subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Inside subgroup (n=6078): tx 0.786 vs ctrl 0.166 (OR=18.42, p<1e-300, abs diff +0.620). Outside subgroup (n=43922): tx 0.160 vs ctrl 0.167 (OR=0.95, p=0.057, abs diff -0.007).

proposed_hypotheses.text: An interaction model with a single in-subgroup indicator captures essentially all treatment-effect heterogeneity for feature_014, i.e., the in-subgroup x feature_014 interaction is large and the feature_014 main effect outside the subgroup is null.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ feature_014 + in_sub + feature_014*in_sub)
analyses.result_summary: Main feature_014 coef (outside subgroup) = -0.054 (p=0.06, near null). in_subgroup main coef = -0.008 (p=0.85, no prognostic effect of subgroup membership in untreated). Interaction = +2.967 (z=39.0, p<1e-300). The 4-feature subgroup x treatment interaction explains essentially all of the treatment effect.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Outside the 4-feature subgroup, no other binary or continuous feature meaningfully modifies the feature_014 treatment effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: For each candidate modifier c, fit Logit(response ~ tx + c + tx*c) on data outside the subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Among feature_004, feature_020, feature_022, feature_024, feature_027, feature_011, feature_010, feature_005, feature_016, feature_001, feature_021, feature_026, feature_009 examined outside the subgroup, no tx*c interaction is significant (smallest p for inter coef = 0.11 for feature_020). Outside-subgroup tx main effect is also small and weakly negative (~ -0.05 log-odds, p>0.04). The 4-feature subgroup is exhaustive for capturing benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_026 and feature_009 are not independently prognostic for objective_response outside the dual-biomarker-positive stratum; their apparent main effects in univariate analysis arise from suppression of the treatment effect within the biomarker-positive group.

analyses.code: Mean response by feature_026 and feature_009 in patients NOT in (feature_001=1 & feature_021=1)
analyses.result_summary: Outside dual-positive: feature_026=0 rr=0.1644 (n=35731) vs feature_026=1 rr=0.1648 (n=6256), no difference. feature_009=0 rr=0.1646 (n=37874) vs feature_009=1 rr=0.1634 (n=4113), no difference. Both features only matter as treatment-effect modifiers when paired with feature_001=1 & feature_021=1.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive 4-feature subgroup, the feature_014 treatment effect remains very large and statistically significant in each level of feature_004.

analyses.code: Stratified Fisher exact for feature_014 within subgroup at each feature_004 level
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup, feature_004=0: tx 0.859 vs 0.216 (OR=22.1, p~1e-173). feature_004=1: tx 0.772 vs 0.149 (OR=19.3, p~1e-239). feature_004=2: tx 0.668 vs 0.107 (OR=16.9, p~1e-66). The treatment effect is large and homogeneous on the OR scale across feature_004 levels (interaction p=0.24).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within the responsive 4-feature subgroup, higher feature_004 reduces both baseline response and treated response, acting as a dose-dependent prognostic modifier on top of the treatment effect.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ feature_014 + feature_004) within 4-feature subgroup
analyses.result_summary: feature_014 coef = +2.980 (p<1e-300), feature_004 coef = -0.475 per level (p<1e-20). Untreated response in subgroup falls from 0.216 (level 0) to 0.107 (level 2); treated falls from 0.859 to 0.668.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Continuous prognostic features (feature_020, feature_024, feature_027, feature_022, feature_029) remain associated with objective_response in untreated patients, supporting that they are baseline prognostic markers and not treatment-effect markers.

analyses.code: Welch t-test on each continuous feature among feature_014=0 only
analyses.result_summary: In untreated arm: feature_020 diff=-0.602 (p~1e-30, lower→response), feature_024 diff=-2.05 (p~1e-14), feature_027 diff=-0.58 (p~1e-7), feature_029 diff=+0.028 (p~1e-5, higher→response), feature_022 diff=-1.27 (p~1e-4). Effects in untreated mirror full-cohort effects, indicating prognostic (baseline) rather than treatment-modifying associations.

proposed_hypotheses.text: feature_014 assignment is approximately balanced (random) with respect to feature_001, feature_021, feature_026, feature_009, and feature_004, consistent with a randomized controlled trial design.

analyses.code: Cross-tabulate feature_014 with each candidate biomarker; compute proportions
analyses.result_summary: Prevalence of biomarker=1 among feature_014=0 vs feature_014=1: feature_001 0.402 vs 0.399; feature_021 0.400 vs 0.401; feature_026 0.148 vs 0.151; feature_009 0.099 vs 0.100; feature_004 distribution near identical. No imbalance - assignment is random.

proposed_hypotheses.text: No other binary feature among feature_010, feature_005, feature_016, feature_025, feature_019, feature_015, feature_018, feature_008, feature_006, feature_032, feature_030 acts as a treatment-like factor with a feature_001 x feature_021 interaction comparable to feature_014.

analyses.code: For each candidate t in {feature_010, ...}, fit Logit(response ~ t + (feature_001*feature_021) + t*(feature_001*feature_021))
analyses.result_summary: Only feature_014 (and trivially feature_001, feature_021 themselves) show large positive interactions with the dual-biomarker indicator. feature_026 and feature_009 show negative interactions (consistent with suppressing the feature_014-driven effect via correlated subgroup definition) but no other binary candidate has a positive interaction. feature_014 is the unique active treatment.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The proportion of patients eligible to benefit from feature_014 (i.e., the responsive subgroup) is approximately 12% of the cohort.

Among those eligible by biomarker (feature_001=1 & feature_021=1 & feature_026=0 & feature_009=0), absolute response rate is increased from ~17% to ~79% by feature_014, an absolute gain of approximately 62 percentage points.

analyses.code: Count of subgroup membership
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup size: 6078 / 50000 = 12.2% of patients. Treated within subgroup: 1790 (29.5% of subgroup); untreated within subgroup: 4288.

analyses.code: Compare response rates within subgroup by treatment
analyses.result_summary: Within subgroup: untreated response 0.166 (n=4288) vs treated response 0.786 (n=1790). Absolute risk difference +0.620; risk ratio 4.73; OR 18.42, Fisher exact p approaches 0.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Final best-supported subgroup hypothesis: in patients with feature_001=1 AND feature_021=1 AND feature_026=0 AND feature_009=0, feature_014=1 produces a large positive effect on objective_response (absolute risk increase ~+62 percentage points; OR ~18); in any patient violating any of these four predicates, feature_014 has no effect on objective_response.

analyses.code: Logit(response ~ tx + subgroup_indicator + tx*subgroup_indicator) on all data
analyses.result_summary: Treatment-by-subgroup interaction OR=exp(2.967)=19.4, p<1e-300. Outside subgroup, feature_014 effect log-odds=-0.054 (OR=0.95, p=0.06). Within subgroup, feature_014 effect log-odds=-0.054+2.967=+2.913 (OR~18.4). Subgroup membership has no prognostic effect among untreated (log-odds=-0.008, p=0.85). The four-predicate subgroup definition is the complete and parsimonious description of the treatment effect.
